title: Check eligibility
explanation: You must be an Indian citizen, at least 18 years old on the qualifying date, and ordinarily resident in the polling area.
beginnerNote: Your 18th birthday is the magic number. If you turn 18 on or before January 1st (or the other qualifying dates), you can apply.

title: Register as voter
explanation: Fill out Form 6. You can do this completely online via the Voters' Portal or Voter Helpline App, or physically submit it to your Electoral Registration Officer.
beginnerNote: Keep a recent passport-sized photo, address proof (like an electricity bill), and age proof (like a birth certificate or 10th mark sheet) ready.

title: Check your name in voter list
explanation: Having a Voter ID (EPIC) is not enough. Your name must strictly be on the current Electoral Roll for your constituency.
beginnerNote: Always search your name online a few weeks before the election. If it's missing, you cannot vote.

title: Find polling booth
explanation: Your name is tied to a specific polling station. You can only cast your vote at that designated location.
beginnerNote: You can find the exact address of your polling booth using the Voter Helpline App or SMS services.

title: Carry accepted ID
explanation: To prove your identity, you must carry your original EPIC (Voter ID). If you don't have it, ECI allows alternative photo IDs like Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, or Driving License.
beginnerNote: Photocopies or photos on your phone are not accepted. Take the physical, original card.

title: Vote on polling day
explanation: Go to the booth, get your identity verified, have your finger inked, and press the blue button next to your chosen candidate on the EVM. Check the VVPAT slip.
beginnerNote: The VVPAT is a glass window that shows a printed slip of your vote for 7 seconds. It proves your vote was recorded correctly.

title: Understand result day
explanation: Votes are counted under strict surveillance. The candidate with the highest number of valid votes in your constituency is declared the winner.
beginnerNote: Even if your chosen candidate doesn't win, the winning candidate becomes the representative for your entire area.
